summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the period of y = sin x?

The basic sine function y = sin x completes one full cycle every 2π units.

What is the amplitude of y = 3sin x?

The amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient: |3| = 3.

What is the period of y = cos(2x)?

Period = 2π/|b| = 2π/2 = π.

What is the phase shift of y = sin(x − π/3)?

y = sin(x − c) shifts right by c. Here c = π/3, so shift is π/3 to the right.

What is the vertical shift of y = cos x + 4?

Adding 4 outside the function shifts the entire graph up by 4 units.

What is the range of y = 2sin x?

Amplitude = 2, so the function oscillates between −2 and 2.

What is the period of y = tan x?

The tangent function repeats every π units, unlike sine and cosine which repeat every 2π.

What is the 'amplitude' of a trigonometric function?

Amplitude = (max − min)/2. For y = A sin(Bx), the amplitude is |A|.

What is the 'period' of a trigonometric function?

The period is the smallest positive value T such that f(x + T) = f(x). For y = sin(Bx), period = 2π/|B|.

What is 'phase shift'?

Phase shift is the horizontal displacement of a trig graph. In y = sin(Bx − C), the phase shift is C/B.

What is the 'midline' of a trigonometric function?

The midline is y = D, the average of the max and min. The graph oscillates above and below this line.

What is 'frequency' in trigonometry?

Frequency = 1/period = |B|/(2π). It tells how many cycles the function completes per unit interval.

What is a 'vertical asymptote' in the context of y = tan x?

tan x has vertical asymptotes at x = π/2 + nπ where cos x = 0 and tan x is undefined.

What is the domain of y = sin x?

Sine is defined for all real numbers. Its domain is (−∞, ∞).

What is an 'asymptote'?

An asymptote is a line that the graph gets arbitrarily close to but typically does not cross. Tangent has vertical asymptotes.

A sound wave has amplitude 5 and frequency 440 Hz. If modeled as y = 5sin(2π·440·t), what is the period?

Period = 1/frequency = 1/440 seconds. This is the time for one complete vibration.

The tide at a harbor varies sinusoidally. High tide is 12 feet at 6 AM, low tide is 4 feet at 12 PM. What is the amplitude?

Amplitude = (max − min)/2 = (12 − 4)/2 = 4 feet.

A Ferris wheel has a diameter of 50 feet and its center is 30 feet off the ground. It completes one revolution every 2 minutes. What is the equation for a rider's height h(t)?

Amplitude = 25 (radius), midline = 30 (center height), period = 2 min so B = 2π/2 = π. h(t) = 25sin(πt) + 30.

The temperature in a city varies sinusoidally from a low of 40°F in January to a high of 90°F in July. What is the midline temperature?

Midline = (max + min)/2 = (90 + 40)/2 = 65°F.

An AC voltage is modeled by V(t) = 170sin(120πt). What is the maximum voltage and frequency?

Amplitude = 170V (max voltage). B = 120π, so period = 2π/(120π) = 1/60 s, frequency = 60 Hz.

Daylight hours in a city vary sinusoidally. The maximum is 15 hours (June 21) and minimum is 9 hours (Dec 21). What is the amplitude of this model?

Amplitude = (15 − 9)/2 = 3 hours.

A pendulum swings with displacement θ(t) = 0.2cos(3t) radians. What is the maximum angular displacement and the period?

Amplitude = 0.2 radians (max displacement). Period = 2π/3 seconds.

A heartbeat monitor shows a periodic signal with period 0.8 seconds. What is the heart rate in beats per minute?

Frequency = 1/0.8 = 1.25 beats/sec. Beats per minute = 1.25 × 60 = 75.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: An AC voltage is modeled by V(t) = 170sin(120πt). What is the maximum voltage and frequency?

Amplitude = 170V (max voltage). B = 120π, so period = 2π/(120π) = 1/60 s, frequency = 60 Hz.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

What is 'frequency' in trigonometry

Frequency = 1/period = |B|/(2π). It tells how many cycles the function completes per unit interval.

A sound wave has amplitude 5 and frequency 440 Hz. If modeled as y = 5sin(2π·440·t), what is the period

Period = 1/frequency = 1/440 seconds. This is the time for one complete vibration.

An AC voltage is modeled by V(t) = 170sin(120πt). What is the maximum voltage and frequency

Amplitude = 170V (max voltage). B = 120π, so period = 2π/(120π) = 1/60 s, frequency = 60 Hz.
